§6Tritium§f

§aRadioactive hydrogen isotope — fusion fuel, reactor rods, and glowing lamps!§f

§bTritium§f (T, Hydrogen-3) is a flammable, combustible, radioactive gas (NFPA 3/4/0) with high fuel grade (5 kTU, 10 kHE per bucket). Used primarily in the fusion reactor with deuterium.

§6Production§f (§bRBMK Irradiation Channel§f):
§b• Block of Lithium§f → §e10,000 mB§f Tritium
§b• Lithium Powder§f → §e1,000 mB§f Tritium
§b• Tiny Pile of Lithium Powder§f → §e100 mB§f Tritium

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• §eMakeshift Energy Core§f — Advanced Alloy + Superconducting Coil + Tungsten + Deuterium Cell + Tritium Cell
§b• §eCustom Nuke Hydrogen Rod§f — Lead Plate + Iron Plate + Tritium Cell
§b• §eTritium Lamps§f — Glass + Red Phosphorus + Tritium Tank + Sulfur → §aGreen Lamp§f; Glass + Red Phosphorus + Tritium Tank + Aluminium Powder → §bBlue Lamp§f
§b• §eFusion Reactor§f — combined with deuterium for fusion fuel
§b• §eZIRNOX Rods§f — tritium-enriched fuel rods

§eTip§f: Lithium is the key input. Set up RBMK irradiation channels with blocks of lithium for bulk tritium production. Each block yields 10,000 mB — enough for 10 tritium cells. The tritium lamps are a nice decorative byproduct!
